title: narcissus triandrus var. macleayi
intro: Narcissus triandrus var. macleayi, also known as Malleeflower Jonquil, is a variety of the species Narcissus triandrus, which is a type of perennial bulbous plant native to Western Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Narcissus triandrus var. macleayi include its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy for colds and flu. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture, and is often used to treat respiratory issues and fever in traditional Western Australian medicine.
